name: Monarch Butterfly
description: Milkweed butterfly in the family Nymphalidae
summary: The monarch butterfly or simply monarch is a milkweed butterfly in the family Nymphalidae. Other common names, depending on region, include milkweed, common tiger, wanderer, and black-veined brown. It is among the most familiar of North American butterflies and an iconic pollinator, although it is not an especially effective pollinator of milkweeds. Its wings feature an easily recognizable black, orange, and white pattern, with a wingspan of 8.9–10.2 cm (3.5–4.0 in). A Müllerian mimic, the viceroy butterfly, is similar in color and pattern, but is markedly smaller and has an extra black stripe across each hindwing.

wikipedia_sections.etymology: Many monarch researchers have cited recent evidence from long-term citizen science data that shows that the number of breeding (adult) monarchs has not declined in the last two decades. [ 200 ] [ 201 ] [ 202 ] The lack of long-term declines in the numbers of breeding and migratory monarchs, yet the clear declines in overwintering numbers, suggests a growing disconnect exists between these life stages. One researcher has suggested that mortality from car strikes constitutes an increasing threat to migrating monarchs. Mexican environmental authorities continue to monitor illegal logging of the oyamel fir trees ; however, organized criminals have repeatedly crushed such efforts in the name of very short-term financial gain.

wikipedia_sections.threats: [ 203 ] A study of road mortality in northern Mexico, published in 2019, showed very high mortality from just two "hotspots" each year, amounting to 200,000 monarchs killed. The decline was expected to increase during the 2013–2014 season. wikipedia_sections.conservation: plexippus , eastern migratory population) in their overwintering areas in Mexico between 1993 and 2018 The area of Mexican forest to which eastern and midwestern monarchs migrate reached its lowest level in two decades in 2013. The strategy laid out federal actions to achieve three goals, two of which were: Monarch Butterflies: Increase the Eastern population of the monarch butterfly to 225 million butterflies occupying an area of approximately 15 acres (6.1 hectares) in the overwintering grounds in Mexico, through domestic/international actions and public-private partnerships, by 2020.

physical.lifespan: 2-6 weeks after becoming an adult, 8-9 months for the last generation of the year

ecology.habitat: Sunny places with streams, vegetation, and few predators (wintering habitat)
ecology.locations: Africa, Asia, Central-America, Europe, North-America, Oceania, South-America
distinctive_features: Black, orange, and white pattern on wings

reproduction.gestation_period: 3 to 8 days for eggs to hatch after they are laid

additional_info.most_distinctive_feature: Black, orange, and white pattern on wings
